desc:
The plains of Eriador. Most have heard of them, as they cover most of the
country, but few visit. Small settlements such as Bree exist like small islands
on a barren sea. Many years ago the Dunedain could be found here, but since the
defeat of Arthedain, the area has become desolate and in some places dangerous.
name: Plains

desc:
You have moved far into the plains. All around the green grass stretches towards
the horizon. It would be easy to lose your bearings in these fields, but you do
seem to recall the road being somewhere south of you.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing in the middle of the wide plains that reach far into the
distance. You can barely see your feet as you make your way through. As the
gentle wind bends the grass tracks of different animals can be seen, both
smaller and larger.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing in a wide plain that stretches north to the horizon. The dry
grass grows up to your waist and slight waves form on it in the gentle breeze.
Here and there grows a single shrubbery and you can see tracks of several hoofed
animals, as well as from lesser animals, such as rabbits.
name: Plains

desc:
Though the lands about are flat, the travel is harsh. Cracks created by the lack
of moisture cross the land, providing an inconvenience. Additionally, the strong
winds which blow without remorse, buffet the terrain. The air they carry is dry
and lacks any scent, as the only plants are occasional grasses.
name: Plains

desc:
Pot-holes and sunken streambeds dot the flat land. The unforgiving soil supports
little plant growth and even fewer animals. Water soaks quickly underground, but
whence it goes is a mystery.
name: The Cracked Land

desc:
Onwards and on the road lies, while all about the flat plains of the En
Eredoriath offer little prospect. The stone-edged highway extends for many
leagues in either direction, seemingly without end.
name: The Old East Road

desc:
You are standing on the Old East Road going east and west from here through the
endless dry plains. A few bushes and a lonely tree grow by the way here but
mainly there is desolation.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Keeping a respectful distance, this underused trail tracks the course of the
hedge of Cardolan. For much of its great length, one knows the hedge follows the
southern side of the East Road. However, to the east this partnership must end,
as the hedge veers southeast towards Eregion.
name: The Hedge-trail

desc:
This is the overgrown remains of an old track running parallel with the great
hedge to the north. The signs are that it is still in use, but it suffers from a
lack of maintenance for many a year. Here, the trail wanders west and bends
south, cutting a path through the miles of broken grassland. Following this
trail south takes you towards a fortress standing out against the horizon.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
As you examine the grass closer you are able to discern what appears to be a
trail running north and south. Looking south you realise that the trail leads
from the road to a castle, a few miles away. To the east and west the plains
stretch out in an endless sea of swaying grass.
name: Plains

desc:
Within an endless expanse of overgrown grass, you journey. The geography seems
pretty constant for miles around. To the north, not far away, you espy the
reassuring sight of the old east road, making its way through the miles of
desolation that is the lone-lands.
name: Plains

desc:
On this side of the Weather Hills the ground is flat as far as the eye can see.
The grass growing here is short and tough, and there is not a tree for miles
around. The impressive thorny bushes of the hedgewall lie south of this ground,
forming an impenetrable barrier to all travellers. To the north you can still
see the Old East Road.
name: Grasslands

desc:
Within sight of the East Road, lies the remains of the marker which denoted the
meeting point of the three sister realms after the division of Arnor: Arthedain
and its former stronghold at Amon Sul to the northwest; the downs of Cardolan to
the south and west; and the endless plains of Rhudaur to the east. In the time
of the Dunedain kingdoms, this marker was known as the Seroneldeon Tarma or
Three-friends' Pillar. After the wars which broke the kingdoms, travellers
passing along the road have referred to the ruins as the Folly of the North or
more commonly: The Three-fools' Stone.
name: The Three-fools' Stone

desc:
The trail is old and poorly maintained. Vegetation from both sides of the trail
have been allowed to grow freely, leaving only a small stretch of track still
passable. Bordering the road to the south are vast green plains.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
This is the plains of the Lone-lands, where little grows and little feeds on
that growth. Most people pass through this area quickly, not tarrying on their
route as there are few distractions.
name: Sparse Plains

desc:
This trail half an age ago would have marked the eastern border of the former
Dunedain kingdom of Cardolan. Directly to the north of the path stands the great
hedge which served as a defence. To this day, it is still practically
impassable. Beyond the thorns, to the north-west stands Weathertop, another part
of the boundary, where all three of the Arnorian realms met. Given the many
centuries, this trail surprisingly remains usable, though the stones which
support it are in need of much repair. Looking east, the trail passes just to
the north of an old castle. To the south and west the harsh landscape of the
plains stretches for miles.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
You find yourself in grass up to your waist and it's quite hard to walk. Bending
some of the grass aside you discern tracks of several small animals. The trail
runs north and east of here.
name: Plains

desc:
The weather-beaten old trail makes its way through the endless plains of rocky
grasses, following the southern edge of the thorny hedgewall of Cardolan. At
this point, the hedge to the north has been burned and cleared and a wide path
is now possible north. Beyond the hedge, the most prominant sight is the great
hill of Amon Sul. To the south, a cleared road leads from the trail to the gates
of a castle. Many tracks on the ground show that the fortress is still in use.
name: An Old Trail by a Fortress

desc:
The cobbled trail makes its way through the great grassy plains of the
lone-lands, making the journey that bit more easy. However, in several places
the track is in danger of being lost as the years have taken their toll. A
castle is visible south-west of here and that is one of the only features in
this grassy landscape. The wind blows strong in your face and their is no tree
or bush nearby to shelter from its fierceness bar the great line of thorns
directly north of the path..
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
You are following the remains of the Dunedain trail of former Cardolan. This
inhospitable reach of the realm may still find use with the shepherds but it is
unlikely. The grass grows across the stones of the trail, threatening to swallow
it forever.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
The grass is quite low around here, lower than usual. It's strangely quiet here
and the sky seems nearer than you've ever experienced it before. Looking
westwards you notice a way through the great hedge. The grass also appears
trampled in that direction.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing just south of some brushwood. The vegetation is truly
prospering here and the grass is tall and green. Sounds of animals scurrying
around reaches your ears.
name: Plains

desc:
The grass is dry and brownish and cracks under your feet. It would be hard to
move around without leaving obvious tracks here. Scanning your surroundings
reveals nothing but endless plains, a small patch of brush to your east and of
course the road just north of you.
name: Plains

desc:
Here you have become entangled in a patch of thorny bushes which grow here. The
bushes form a dense weave, and it is with effort that you struggle to move
through. To the north, the plains clear to form the old east road, but in other
directions there is just endless plains.
name: A Patch of Brush

desc:
Plains border the Old East Road which lie north of here, clearly visible beyond
the swaying grass. South of you the vegetation is more sparse as the ground
becomes increasingly rough and unfriendly. Tracks of small animals is visible in
the dirt.
name: Plains

desc:
You find yourself knee-deep in a sea of swaying grass. The plains border the
road which runs north and east of here. More plains are visible west- wards and
a craggy, rougher landscape to the south.
name: Plains

desc:
The road bends again, offering to take you east and north from here. Looking
south and west you see vast plains of tall green grass. Such vegetation is
likely to harbour many small creatures for a hungry hunter.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Sharp rocks and thorny bushes makes it harder to walk here. Not much grass grows
between the rocks. To the west and north however, the vegetation is more dense.
The rough grounds continue to the east and south.
name: Rough Grounds

desc:
You carefully squeeze your way between two bushes and walk around some large
rocks. The place is rather weird and it seems easy to get lost here among the
many rocks but to your southeast you can see the remains of an old crumbling
castle.
name: Rough Grounds

desc:
The grass appears somewhat trampled here; studying the ground more carefully
reveals some tracks leading to and from the road. Apparently some travellers
before you stepped off the road here, perhaps to rest some before continuing
their journey.
name: Plains

desc:
You carefully squeeze your way between two bushes and walk around some large
rocks. The place is rather weird and it would be easy to get lost among the many
rocks. It's hard to move fast without cutting or hurting oneself on the sharp
surfaces of the rocks.
name: Rough Grounds

desc:
Squeezing your way through a couple of bushes you make your way forward. Small
patches of grass grow between the unfriendly rocks that dominate this place. To
the east some grassy plains are visible.
name: Rough Grounds

desc:
Making your way through the high grass is not as easy as you first thought. It
is difficult to maintain a sense of direction when so much of what you see is
the same. Westwards however, the vegetation becomes less dense.
name: Plains

desc:
The grass grows high here, yet it seems dull and lifeless. The ground lacks any
topsoil, and rain that falls here is quickly gathered in small brooks among the
faint low hills, draining the plains. Stepping over one of these brooks you
notice a very round and white rock on the bottom. North and west of here the
grass retains its thickness and height, but east and south it grows shorter and
more sparse for a few miles. A thick green-grey streak lines the horizon in
these directions, indicating denser vegetation.
name: Plains

desc:
Running in parallel with the road, these plains show many tracks of travellers
having stepped off the road. The plains continue in what appears to be endless
fields to the east and to the west. Examining the ground reveals tracks of both
smaller creatures and of larger, hoofed animals.
name: Plains

desc:
The road goes on east and west as far as you can see with plains on both sides.
To the east, the grass starts to give way to a much thicker brush, the old east
road cutting its way through.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road runs eastwards and westwards as far as the eye can see. Bordering the
road is what appears to be endless plains of swaying grass. Sounds of small
animals moving around in the grass reaches your ears.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The ground which constitutes the road is trampled and shows many tracks of
hoofed animals, probably horses. To the north and south vast plains of swaying
grass invites you to step off the road and rest some before continuing your
journey.
name: Old East Road

desc:
To the south you can see some kind of fortification, though it lies many miles
from here. The immediate surroundings are marked mainly by the road, which cuts
through a vast, seemingly endless plain.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You stir up some dust as you make your way forward. Small pebbles lie on the
sides of the road. The sky is high above and you feel tired of your long
journey. Perhaps you should take a rest before heading any further.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road continues east and west from here, going through the endless plains
which dry grass swaying in the wind makes you feel you are journeying through an
ocean without a boat.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road goes on east and west again seemingly forever surrounded by the endless
green plains. Some low bushes are growing beside it.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road bends here, continuing to the west and south. Plains border the road in
all directions. The dirt is hard and trampled, and pebbles are scattered all
over the road. On a surface like this, you are certain not to leave as many
tracks as someone moving beside the road.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road stretches westwards and eastwards in what seems to be an endless effort
to reach some unknown goal, always inviting travellers to use it to reach their
destination. You are standing in an ocean a vast plains, the brown dirt of the
road being the only solid surface in sight.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road winds away east and west before you like an ancient snake whose scales
have begun to erode and rot from the passage of time. The stone tiles of the
road are broken and scattered, with brown weeds and grass fighting for the
patches of ground now uncovered. The land here is flat, but covered with many
spiney shrubs and dark thorny brush. The wind constantly blows dust into the
air, choking your throat and tearing at your eyes. An eerie silence hangs over
this place.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Making its miserable path through this blighted land, the road stretches east
and west towards the horizon. Shadows flit across the road and hawklike cries
pierce the air; you feel strange eyes upon you from above. To the north and
south of the road extends a brown, trackless expanse of thorny brush. The bushes
are taller than a tall man, and obscure all details of the landscape.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The sounds of your boots padding upon the ancient broken tiles of the road rings
in your ears as you wander along the road. Here it bends north and west around a
dark cropping of brush and trees and becomes lost in the haze and distance of
the plains. The wind silently blows into the thorny hedges which dot the road,
making them wave as if they were either calling you or repelling you. You do not
know which. The squawk of a swooping vulture alerts you that no place here is
safe. You want to keep moving.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road winds east to south here through a seemingly endless sea of thorny
brush. The bushes rise up on both sides of the road like walls, unnaturally
tall, unnaturally deadly. The air is quiet and heavy; there is little sign of
animal life. The squeaks of rats, and maybe of something else, can be heard in
the thickets.
name: Old East Road

desc:
These brushlands punish your sight with harsh views of desolation broken only by
forbidding brush and the occasional tree. You wonder why you left the lush
greens of your homeland for these hard browns and greys which now assail you.
`How can anything live here?', you ask yourself, but you do not know. And you do
not want to know.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Like a faithful guide, the road leads east and west through these brushlands
that surround you. You cautiously do not stray even an inch off the well beaten
path for, though you see and hear nothing, you are sure that out there,
something is watching you. You feel safe upon this old pathway, where thousands
have tread in time lost in memory but you wonder: Can it protect you from
whatever lies hidden in the brush that is just out of sight? The thorny bushes
are particularly tall on both sides of the road here.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You continue to trudge within the comforting boundaries of the road which curves
east and south here. Only the smell of death assaults your nose as you come
across the skeletal remains of some animal, the like of which you have never
seen in your journeys across Arda. You do not want to become a trophy to the
victory of this brush over your will, you are determined to move on and keep
moving until it is behind you. But there is nothing to be seen on any side save
great brown thorny bushes and the tops of some of the taller trees which poke up
out of the brush like the heads of drowning men.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You follow the road which can take you either east or west from here though
neither direction seems to be able to rid you of this infernal brushland with
its thorny bushes, tall, rotting fields of brown weed, and dark, mottled earth.
This place breathes rot and ruin upon you and you wish to be done with it.
Though you walk heavily and your weapons and pack clatter together, you think
you distinctly hear a crunching sound, like leaves being crushed under foot,
coming from behind the brushline to the north.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The old road straggles on to the east and the west, passing through a plain
blotted and choked with dark, evil looking brush and brown weeds. The skyline is
only occasionally broken by a solitary dark, gnarled tree, and the sounds of
life so familiar to you seem stolen from this place. No living animal, save for
circling scavengers above, can be seen anywhere.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Looking like a beaten dog, the road whimpers west and south away from you.
Again, strange noises reach your ears and you could swear you see shapes moving
in the brush to the east and north along the road, where it grows in a great
brown thorny sea. Yet it is hard to imagine what could move in so thick a tangle
of thorny canes and creepers.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. `Where has the road gone?', you ask yourself as you scan the brush for
any sign of the road, but unless your eyes decieve you, you find no sign of the
road. It is as if the Old East Road never was.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
Huge thorn-covered bushes and shrubs rise on every side, greater and more
dangerous-looking than any product of unaided nature. The dagger-like thorns
they bear are fully five inches long, brown, and needle-sharp. The thickets and
brush hide the surrounding landscape from view; only a circle of sky overhead
remains unobscured. Rustling sounds can be heard from the depths of the brush.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
The grass grows low here, and has a brownish tone. In large areas nothing grows
at all, exposing white hard soil. This landscape continues south, while the
grass grows thicker and higher in the other directions. Far west-northwest you
see the blue shadow of Amon Sul on the horizon. For a long time it has been
watching these plains, and a feeling that someone is searching you right now
from that direction comes over you.
name: Chalk Plains

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it.
name: Chalk Plains

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. Here,
the flats fade into a wall of brush in all directions but west, where the flats
linger on.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
This is flat, dryish land which is covered by brown thorny bushes for miles
around. Their creepers seem to wave strangely in the flat air and to tear
maliciously at you. Tall, dense, and opaque, their branches limit your view of
your surroundings and impede passage.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slowly and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. Scanning the expanse of grey plains for some sort of landmark, you
realise to your suprise and dismay that you can no longer find the familier
highway upon which you had just left. All around you, as far as you can see are
plains and scrubs, weeds, and dark trees, but....no road.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. `Where has the road gone?', you ask yourself as you scan the brush for
any sign of the road, but unless your eyes decieve you, you find no sign of the
road. It is as if the Old East Road never was.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
This is a lonely and infertile land of stunted trees, brown bushes, unhealthy-
looking thickets, and mud flats. There is little sign of life. Perhaps it is
true that this was fertile land once, hundreds of years ago, as the scholars
say; but no trace of civilisation remains that you can see.
name: In the Brush

desc:
The grassy plains come to an abrupt end here as tougher vegetation eastwards
prevent the sunlight from reaching the ground. Not much can be seen from within
the brush, but sounds of moving creatures reaches your ears. To the north the
Old East Road is clearly visible. South of here, wide white plains begin. Far
away beyond these, a green line is visible below the horizon - the Great Hedge.
name: Plains

desc:
Little can be seen or perceived here beyond the thorny net of brush which
enshrouds this spot. The open sky above seems distant, fragile; indeed some
whiplike thorny withes extend across it, linking some of the tree-high
thornbushes around which you must navigate. The soil is mucky and brown, and
fallen thorny branches reach up to tear your feet.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. As you look around, you can no longer see the road from which you just
departed. And though you can see clearly the brush around you, and the plains
beyond, the Old East Road seems to have simply vanished.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
Great stands of brown brush, its canes armed with sharp, tearing thorns, rise up
all around, almost hiding from view some foundation stones and crumbled,
overgrown walls. Perhaps this was a fortification once, but it is impossible
really to tell. The grey stones have lost their corners, and resemble a
scattered heap of frost-worn boulders more than the remains of any definite type
of building. Muddy water trickles southward at the base of the stones.
name: Brush-covered Stones

desc:
Thorny bushes all but block the light of day, growing unnaturally tall - as high
as fifteen feet - and bearing broad, heavy branches covered with ripping thorns.
The lay of the land can only be sensed, not seen. It rises sharply to the east,
while sloping gently downward to the west where a muddy trickle wanders slowly
among the bases of the huge bushes. Odd footprints can be seen in the mud, and
the squeak of rats can be heard in the brush all around.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
This valley is shallow, broad, and bounded by low embankments to the east and
west. If it were not for the huge thornbushes which fill virtually every square
foot of its floor, obscuring the small, muddy southward-tending stream from
view, and which block and overhang every possible place where the embankments
might be climbed, travel through here would not be difficult. In fact, it is
very difficult to get out of the valley on either side. The thorns seem to
positively resist, interlacing themselves to block passage.
name: Thornbush-choked Valley

desc:
This is the valley of a small southward-tending creek, muddy and nearly dry,
which dribbles slowly through a succession of muddy pools. The valley is
virtually choked with tall, brown-withed thorny bushes, whose tangled canes make
travel almost impossible, particularly up the embankments to the east and west
which are overhung by the huge and malevolent plants.
name: Brushy Valley

desc:
This is a valley that trends northward and southward, at the lowest point of
which a muddy stream, or trickle, flows at a snail's pace from one muddy puddle
to another. The land is rocky and harsh, and it is hard to believe that this
stream actually formed the valley; probably it is just the lowest point in these
shallow hills. The valley is choked by the dense thorny bushes which surround
you on all sides, and it is very difficult to get any sense of the lay of the
land.
name: Brush-filled Swale

desc:
You wander into a patch of shrubs dominated by dark gnarled trees whose warped
and twisted branches stretch all around, forming a canopy which blocks out all
external light. As you move, you step on old blackened leaves which crunch
loudly as you tread upon them and dark crows scatter from their shelter as you
approach.
name: Dark Clump of Trees

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
This is a sea of thornbushes - more trackless than any sea, for there is no mast
to climb, no crow's-nest from which to take a bearing. Struggling through these
bushes, which tower over you, surround you, and let you advance not an inch
without exacting its toll in scratches and sweat, is perhaps more like
travelling under water might be. Certainly the heavy air seems no more fresh or
life-giving than swamp water might be.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. `Where has the road gone?', you ask yourself as you scan the brush for
any sign of the road, but unless your eyes decieve you, you find no sign of the
road. It is as if the Old East Road never was.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it.
name: In the Brush

desc:
There is no path, there is no vacant ground, there is no open space - just an
endless thicket of tall, tearing, grasping thorny brush. The ground is a
pincushion of fallen tangled thorn-covered withes. The sky is a distant glimmer,
half-obscured by netted creepers. The air is moist, musty, and heavy, and smells
of rot. Out of sight in the depths of the tangles, strange squeaks, rustles, and
movements can barely be discerned.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
Great bramble bushes the size of apple trees huddle together, merge, coalesce
into a single monstrous brambly thicket. There is no telling where one bush ends
and another begins; whether some ephemeral chink of vacant space is between or
within. There is only a continuous mass of briars, of thorny creepers, of muddy
thorns underfoot and tearing, ripping thorns at eye level. The air is dense and
stagnant, yet there is movement in the depths of the bushes, and sounds:
squeaking, rustling, gurgling.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. `Where has the road gone?', you ask yourself as you scan the brush for
any sign of the road, but unless your eyes deceive you, you find no sign of the
road. It is as if the Old East Road never was.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
The land here is one continuous thicket of tall thorny bushes, obstructing
travel and vision alike. The ground is grey-brown and worn, composed of dried
muck cementing together fallen thorny twigs and canes. A heavy silence prevails,
broken only by the occasional lonely call of a bird of prey, or by a rustling in
the brush, somewhere beyond the interlaced wall of branches.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
The ground beneath you is cracked and dry. The flora is a variety of greys and
browns, and is thick and thorny. What little grass there is is dried and a
sickly yellow. The land is quiet, except for the rustle of some twigs as you
pass on top of them. The Old East Road travels east-west just south of here.
name: In the Brush

desc:
The plains border the road which runs to the west and south of here. Some animal
tracks can be seen on the ground. A group of trees surrounded by a shrubbery
grows here. The sky above you seems further away than ever.
name: Plains

desc:
You are walking through the high grass of some flat plains. The plains seem to
go on forever as far as the eye can see. It is easy to get lost in these areas,
but you are fairly sure that the road is somewhere south of you.
name: Plains

desc:
You are walking through the high grass of some flat plains. The plains seem to
go on forever as far as the eye can see. It is easy to get lost in these areas,
but you are fairly sure that the road is somewhere south of you.
name: Plains

desc:
Surrounded by plains in all directions you begin to wonder if it wouldn't be
better to head back to the road again. The grass is dry and cracks under your
feet. A closer examination of the ground reveals some tracks of hoofed creatures
and tiny footprints of unknown origin.
name: Plains

desc:
Surrounded by plains it is hard to keep track of your whereabouts. You are
however sure of the fact that the road is somewhere to the south. Every once in
a while a small animal can be seen through the grass. This would definitely be a
good place for the hungry hunter to find prey.
name: Plains

desc:
The dry grass grows up to your waist and slight waves form on it in the gentle
breeze. Small dried bushes grow here and there and the grass has a less green
look to it. Plains surround you in all directions.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing in the middle of the wide plains that reaches to the horizon in
all directions. The dry grass grows up to your waist and slight waves form on it
in the gentle breeze. Here and there grows a single shrubbery and you can see
tracks of several hoofed animals, as well as from lesser animals, such as
rabbits.
name: Plains

desc:
The grass strokes your body as you move further into the high vegetation. The
dirt is soft and moist and certain to preserve your footprints for a long time.
Looking around you notice that you have lost sight of the road. Nothing is to be
seen except tall green grass all around.
name: Plains

desc:
You are walking on quiet plains, going as far as you can see in all directions.
The grass is dry and high and sways gently in the breeze, forming waves that
make you feel as if you are standing in the middle of a dry ocean.
name: Plains

desc:
Suddenly you find yourself in an area of burnt and damaged vegetation. The burnt
grass and the ashes are signs of a recent brushfire raging these plains. Here
and there fresh grass has sprung through the ashes on their way to once again
cover the ground with tall green grass.
name: Plains

desc:
A casual chirp from a bird in the sky reaches your ears. The tall, dry grass
strokes your waistline as you move. Scanning your surroundings yields nothing
but plains in all directions except to the south, where the Old East Road is
clearly visible.
name: Plains

desc:
A few lonely trees border the road to the south. The dry grass cracks under your
feet as you make your way forward. Sounds of animals can be heard from within
the dense plains. Judging from the many tracks in the dirt this might be a good
place to hunt for food.
name: Plains

desc:
You are walking through the dry grass on some plains beside the Old East road.
The vegetation is sparse and consists mostly of a few bushes and lonely trees
besides the high grass. The silence is uncanny and you cannot see a single
animal around, except for a casual bird flying high in the air.
name: Plains

desc:
In the middle of these plains the grass is burnt and trampled. Someone obviously
made a campfire here quite recently. You are surrounded by grassy plains in all
directions, except to the south, where the road you left is visible. Staying in
these plains might be a good way to elude followers.
name: Plains

desc:
Here, just north of the Old East Road, vast plains of green grass dominates the
surroundings. The grass appears trampled in some places indicating some larger
creatures have walked here recently. It is possible to move in all directions
from here.
name: Plains

desc:
The tall grass growing here offers good protection from the eyes of predators
and other evil-minded creatures. Sounds from small animals running around in the
grass can be heard. Searching for tracks in the dirt also reveals that larger
hoofed animals walked here quite recently.
name: Plains

desc:
Vast green plains border the road that runs just south of you. Looking north you
see tall green grass swaying in the wind. The ground shows signs of various
types of creatures walking here. It is possible to explore the plains further to
the north, east and west.
name: Plains

desc:
As you walk through these silent plains beside the road you notice some strange
clawed tracks in the ground leading northwards to an abandoned farm. Some large
animal unknown to you must have made these tracks.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. Here,
the flats seem to end, disappearing into the weeds to the north and the brush to
the south.
name: Plains

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. Here,
this tract of flat land borders a clump of tall weeds to the north and spiney
shrubs to the south, working its path east and west between them.
name: The Flats

desc:
You are on flat, dry ground, which is so overgrown with tall thornbushes that
your surroundings are completely obscured from view. In every direction they
rise up - perhaps a bit higher to the south and east, but, wherever you turn
your gaze, unnaturally tall and fierce-looking. Their interlaced canes are
covered with huge brown thorns.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
Great stands of thorny brush rise up around you in all directions. Their roots
grasp at the earth like many-knuckled hands; like barbed whips, their creepers
and branches, lined with huge thorns jutting in all directions, rise up all
around, laced together in a sharp and potentially deadly net.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
Thorny creepers tear at you as you pass, ripping and piercing. The largest
thorns are the size of a large man's hand, and are coated with a stinging brown
resin! The bushes coquettishly pull you in, it seems, first giving way, then
rearing up like an impenetrable wall, forbidding and impassable.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
The ground seems stony and infertile; nothing green grows here. Nothing at all
does, save these horrible great thornbushes which blot out the sun and wind in
all directions. Their twisted roots seem to squeeze all the life out of the
ground; their thorny creepers lash at you as you pass, ripping at the exposed
surfaces of your body.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
On every side, in every direction, tall thorny bushes block travel, view, even
the wind. The air is musty and heavy, thick with the smell of rotting
vegetation. There is no path, only occasional chinks in the walls of thorny
brush which tower higher than a tall man. Beyond the lacework of the
sharp-thorned canes, in the shadows, there may have been some movement.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it.
Walking among the plains, you spot a growth of many tall weeds a mile or so to
the west in this otherwise lifeless expanse.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
Tough weeds are thriving aggressively across the field, preventing the ground to
breathe. All around can be seen ahead what was once a field of rye. The ground
is flat on which islands of shrubs and thorny bushes have established
themselves. Sharp rocks are scattered around making it difficult to walk here.
name: Overgrown Plains

desc:
You are standing at an old, crumbled and overgrown farm. The main house can
hardly be seen anymore as only the floor covered with debris remain. Near the
old barn you notice some old man and horse bones.
name: Abandoned Farm

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. Here,
the flats run into a patch of weeds to the east but continue unimpaired to the
west and south.
name: In the Brush

desc:
A sudden opening in the great hedge allows one to pass through the dense
vegetation. Some humanoid tracks, among others, leading north and south are easy
to spot in the grass in between the high bushes. A few miles to the south a
castle rises above the landscape.
name: A Break in the Great Hedge

desc:
This splendid hallway of silver-veined marble goes north-south. Grand pilasters
and ornate statuary adorn the entire length. An elaborate pointed vault,
textured by marble roots, resist the mass of earth sleeping above. Under the
vault, the floor is a complicated weave of flowing mosaic depicting men on a
hill. A few sculptures lie broken but otherwise the area is undamaged.
name: The Vaulted Hallway

desc:
Here behind the wall of the armoury is a secret cache of arms and armour, which
have lain untouched in a long time. Although unremarkable in appearance, the
sealed nature of the stone room has preserved the contents of the room from the
ravages of time, since the seal was last broken.
name: The Hidden Storeroom

desc:
Fallen torso dummies and large racks suggest that this was once a grand armoury.
Now there are few signs of what might have once occupied the shelves, except the
length of the racks. A small hearth in the east wall contains coals and a few
instruments for shaping metal. The paved floor has several man-sized skeletons,
bodies without flesh nor finery.
name: The Remains of an Armoury

desc:
The chamber, once protected by heavy doors to the north, has been breached and
the portals have fallen into this room. Large pieces of furniture have been
smashed in the ensuing conflict, many of them also having the black stain of
ancient blood. All of this must have taken place a long time ago.
name: The Ruined Room

desc:
The racks and cases in this room obviously were former weapon-holders but they
are gone. Locks on several cabinets have been smashed suggesting that the place
was looted. Many man-sized skeletons litter the floor, devoid of any flesh or
apparel. An anvil and cold-drop still remain although there seems to be no
source of heating to complement the process.
name: The Ransacked Weapons-room

desc:
The scene left by this room is all too easy to interpret. Many pieces of
furniture are piled near both of these doors as some kind of makeshift
barricade. They failed. Valiant or desperate, the men fell defending this place.
Skeletons lie scattered between the bunks and debris, some man-size, some more
bow-legged and shorter. The only other worthy feature is the lockers and
cabinets of the room. These have been pried open and their contents removed.
name: Barracks

desc:
Few remarkable features have been placed along this bending section of tunnel
apart from a couple of crude sconces for shedding illumination. The dirty tunnel
is however blocked. Large rocks have either been placed here to shut off the
area beyond, or the tunnel has collapsed under the strain of the rock above. It
is possible to get through, but the passage will be hard won.
name: The Blocked Tunnel

desc:
The grandiose hallway of marble goes north and south. Delicate sculptures adorn
the edge of the walkway, either side of a stunning mosaic on the floor. Ornate
fluted pilasters project from the face of the wall. Above, swirling vines and
creepers of marble flow across the high-pointed arches. Apart from some deep
scratches in the pattern of the floor, this corridor is very well preserved.
name: The Vaulted Hallway

desc:
Throughout this hall, marble clads the walls, fine veins of green and grey
creating a lush environment from harsh stone. Great rock vaulting stretches up
into the heights of the passageway. Upon these upper structures are carved a
jungle of foliage. Small niches containing robust earthen vessels line either
side of the intricate floor mosaic. Between the niches, empty sconces are
attached by curling brackets to the veined-stoned.
name: The Vaulted Hallway

desc:
At this point the great hallway which runs north-south intersects two passages.
Amongst the splendour of the large hall, these are very spartan but functional
exits. The one heading east is roughly circular in cross-section, and in
contrast to the hallway has no outstanding features. To the west, a steep set of
stairs descends sharply, the floor, wall and ceiling of basic stone cladding.
name: The Intersection

desc:
This rough tunnel is in contrast with the hallway with which it intersects to
the west. Devoid of anything more than simple features, the tunnel is cut out of
the hill in a roughly circular cross-section. On the walls, torchbearers are
supported but there is scarcely any wood in them to provide a light.
name: The Dirty Tunnel

desc:
The great marbled hallway continues north and south. Above the height of statues
lining this walkway, an ornate entablature connects the wall with the great
arches of the centre of the corridor. Over the architrave, a wonderfully carved
frieze shows numerous buildings in relief. Principal amongst them is the Citadel
of Fornost and the great tower that once stood above this place. Higher than
this scene is the cornice, consisting of intricate stone scrolling.
name: The Vaulted Hallway

desc:
The great hallway comes to an end before a great entrance. Once this way was
guarded by a huge pair of riveted iron valves. Now they lie forlorn on the
ground between the hall and the chamber beyond. The upper parts of the valves,
which lie in the great room beyond, cannot be made out. A swirling curtain of
darkness clouds that room from one's vision.
name: Before the Great Hall

desc:
This is a huge hall which one can only perceive a small part of. Lying on the
ground are the great doors which once stood in the stone entranceway, their
lower halves beyond the darkness. Framing this entrance is a vast arch that
rises towards the high roof of the chamber. Large slate slabs pave the floor.
name: Entrance to the Great Hall

desc:
This is a huge hall that you can only perceive a small part of. To the north and
west are great carved walls that arch up to high stone struts in the far ceiling
overhead. To the southeast is a black basalt column of stout girth that supports
the vaulting. The floor consists of large slate flagstones.
name: The Great Hall of Celepharn

desc:
This is a huge hall which one can only perceive a small part of. West is the
great, carved wall that arches up to high stone struts in the ceiling overhead.
Towards the centre of the Great Hall are two black basalt columns of stout
girth, which support the vaulting. This vaulting would impress even a dwarf.
name: The Great Hall of Celepharn

desc:
This is a huge hall that you can only perceive a small part of. To the south and
west are great, carved walls that arch up to high stone struts in the far
ceiling overhead. To the northeast is a black basalt column of stout girth that
supports the vaulting. The floor consists of large slate flagstones.
name: The Great Hall of Celepharn

desc:
Central to the southern end of the Great Hall, a sunken floor ten yards in
diameter circles a round depression. From the centre of the Great Hall, a
low-gradient ramp leads down towards this central depression, flanked by a pair
of sable columns. Around the circumference of the dais, the sunken floor is
built to seat an audience facing inwards towards this centre, so obviously
something is missing from the current scene.
name: End of the Great Hall

desc:
This is a huge hall that you can only perceive a small part of. To the north and
west are great, carved walls which arch up to high stone struts in the far
ceiling overhead. To the northwest is a black basalt column of stout girth,
which supports the vaulting. The floor consists of large slate flagstones.
name: The Great Hall of Celepharn

desc:
The huge hall if responsibly lit would be an impressive sight, but in the gloom
its grandeur designed for King Celepharn of Arnor is lost. The stone carvings on
dark granite depict scenes from the lives of the Dunedain nobility. The floor is
more mundane, consisting of large closely fitting flagstones of slate.
name: The Great Hall of Celepharn

desc:
This is a huge hall that you can only perceive a small part of. To the north and
east are great carved walls that arch up to high stone struts in the far ceiling
overhead. To the southwest is a black basalt column of stout girth that supports
the vaulting. The floor consists of large slate flagstones.
name: The Great Hall of Celepharn

desc:
Here in the centre of the Great Hall, one has to struggle to move amongst the
debris. The floor is so covered that the slate cannot be seen below one's feet.
Between the four black pillars that support the vaulting, are strewn debris of
all kinds: wood, rags, rusted mail, broken blades and skeletons of fallen men.
name: Rubbish Heap

desc:
One passes through a sequence of rooms that once stored supplies for the
fortress. Each door in turn has been broken, and a trail of destruction leads
north and south as if some vengeful giant has taken his frustration out on the
inanimate objects.
name: The Storerooms

desc:
Within this enormous glass structure it is very hard to tell one's exact
position. Although the room is lit there is no obvious illumination save the
bright lights that leak through the glass walls and reflect off the mirrored
surfaces. One has to feel in front of oneself to tell where solid structures are
as one's visual perception cannot be trusted to guide.
name: The Maze of Glass and Mirrors

desc:
The length of this gallery has been inlayed with oak panels, dyed a distinctive
crimson. Gilding on the wood depicts a long chain of mountains capped with snow
at their summits. Bathing the chamber with an orange glow, mock suns hanging
from the upper ceiling give the room a volcanic feel. The floor is paved with
irregularly shaped stones of varying types: granite, marble, slate, sandstone
and many more beside. Standing upon a basalt flagstone at the north end of a
gallery, is another of those pillars of marble.
name: The Oak Gallery

desc:
This hallway depicts the standards and crests of a miscellany of institutions
and lesser houses of the Arthadan. How they appear depends mainly on what it is
they represent, be it tapestry, shield or crest. Much of the more perishable
displays have done so, but that leaves several that can be examined. Between the
displays, the western entrance has a caryatid, the statues either side. The
upreaching arms of the stone pair support a thick stone rod. Opposite the
caryatid, a low marble column is the only other notable feature of the hall.
name: The Eastern Hall of Heraldry

desc:
In the confines of this room are stored many crates and boxes, chests and cases:
storage for items from a bygone age. Much of the walls are covered by these, but
where not covered, granite blocks can be seen to dress the walls. Notable is the
fact that in the north part of the ceiling, a block became dislodged and crashed
to the floor, exposing chalk behind. Water seeping in through this breach has
ruined many of the containers here, leaving few intact. In a pool of dirty water
in the centre of the slate floor another of those marble pillars stands, a metal
statue of a large hound atop.
name: The Hold

desc:
This hallway leading east and west is tiled in ceramic of varying hues of blue,
across the floor and halfway up the walls. Above this line, the wall and arched
ceiling are plastered in a cloudy grey, creating a horizon. Mid-way along the
hall, a passage of dark ochre opens up, leading south. Beside this passage
stands a marble column, perhaps a plinth for a statue.
name: The North Hall

desc:
The white plaster on the walls and arches of this room give this vault a stark
appearance, especially as it is lit by four smokeless light-sources hung from
the ceiling. A small pool is sunk into the centre of the floor here, the water
quite cloudy with the years with foam spread across the surface. Standing by the
corner of the pool is a short marble pillar, some two feet in height.
name: The White Vault

desc:
This hall is designed to connect the rooms to the north and south. The walls are
hung with tapestries but most of them have decayed, their detail lost. However,
still hanging from the walls are the crests of the noble houses of Arthedain, a
kingdom of the northern Dunedain. These crests are displayed upon oversized
shields, four on the west wall, three on the east. Between two of the shields on
the east wall an entranceway marks another passage, a caryatid supporting the
low arch. Beside the caryatid stands a low marble column.
name: The Western Hall of Heraldry

desc:
This large chamber is remarkable for its huge hemispherical dome of blue-black
stone. Encrusted into this ceiling are innumerable glowing crystals providing
the illumination of a twilit evening. These crystals show a pattern of stars in
their constellations. The floor, an inky-black sheet of fused glass, seems to
reflect the world above, yet seven stars in a ring shine unusually bright when
compared to the heavens above. Four cardinal exits lead to hallways.
name: The Dome of Constellations

desc:
This walkway is panelled entirely with oiled elm along its entire length. Carved
into the wood are multitudes of nautical scenes, depicting the great ships of
Numenor before it was ruined. Above the walls hang yellow lights that cast
ominous shadows on some of the sections of the wood. A now familiar marble
column has been placed at the north end of the walkway.
name: The Elm Walkway

desc:
This hall connects the shadowy dome to the north to a large hall that runs
east-west. Carved torch-brackets and pegs holding long gowns suggest that some
significant religious event was prepared for here. The floor is a series of
small tiles, illustrating begowned men and women walking south.
name: The Processional Path

desc:
Entering this chamber one is faced with three identical exits leaving east, west
and south. Within the chamber, a single lanthorn hangs from a long metal chain
from the ceiling above. The light from it reflects off an inscribed brass plaque
secured to a flagstone at the centre of the room. Of the identical exits, they
are shrouded in an inky-blackness, leaving little trace of which each would
reveal.
name: Eibon's Chamber

desc:
The long chamber serves as a display room for many pieces of fine crystal and
porcelain. Standing against the walls are several glass-fronted cabinets which
hold the antiques for a future admirer. Unlike the exit to the north and those
to hallways at either end, the southern way is barred by an intricate metal
gate, obscuring the chamber beyond. A low marble column stands beside it.
name: The Long Hallway

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. `Where has the road gone?', you ask yourself as you scan the brush for
any sign of the road, but unless your eyes decieve you, you find no sign of the
road. It is as if the Old East Road never was.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
Always hugging the old road that you follow, hoping it will not lead you astray
to either the east or the north in which directions it flows. But, the mute pain
and stillness of these dark plains are starting to get under skin. You think you
see things where nothing is there, and you begin to hear things from places
where nothing stirs. You want to leave this place. Suddenly, you spot a dark
shadow moving through the brush on the side of the road to the west! You wonder
if this is what had been tracking you all through this place or is it merely
your imagination again?
name: Old East Road

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
Here the battered reminants of the road twist north and west, retreating from a
stand of spiney bushes and dark gnarled trees. You hear no sounds of birds
besides those of carrion birds circling constantly above and you see no animal
but only insects which attack your skin and attempt to blight your food and
water. A sense of foreboding washes over you and you wonder if you will ever
leave this barren place.
name: Old East Road

desc:
You wander into a patch of shrubs dominated by dark gnarled trees whose warped
and twisted branches stretch all around, forming a canopy which blocks out all
external light. As you move, you step on old blackened leaves which crunch
loudly as you tread upon them and dark crows scatter from their shelter as you
approach.
name: Dark Clump of Trees

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You wander into a patch of shrubs dominated by dark gnarled trees whose warped
and twisted branches stretch all around, forming a canopy which blocks out all
external light. As you move, you step on old blackened leaves which crunch
loudly as you tread upon them and dark crows scatter from their shelter as you
approach.
name: Dark Clump of Trees

desc:
Tangled brambles in all directions rising as high as an ordinary housetop - if
there were houses or any sign of civilisation here - obscure the vision and
reduce travel to an utter crawl. There is no path in this muddy earth, though
some odd small footprints can be seen in the mud. Briars tear at the legs, and
thorny canes spring back at the eyes as you struggle through.
name: Thorny Brush
